key_insight: A Fourier neural operator surrogate learns to correct cheaper Boussinesq natural-convection simulations toward high-fidelity compressible-flow results with one fast model evaluation per case.
engineering_relevance: This can reduce the cost of simulating heat-driven flow while preserving much of the accuracy needed for thermal-fluid design and analysis.
method: Generate paired training data from matched Boussinesq and compressible-flow simulations.
Train a Fourier neural operator to map low-fidelity fields to high-fidelity corrections.
Implement both solvers in parallel with an implicit monolithic mixed FEM framework in FEniCSx.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation against COMSOL and literature benchmarks, with SSIM close to 1 and mean-squared error reductions of about one to nearly three orders of magnitude in 2D and 3D tests.
watch_item: Generalization depends on the quality and coverage of paired training data, and the approach still requires reliable high-fidelity reference simulations for dataset generation.
paper_title: A Neural Surrogate Approach for Simulating Natural Convection Problems
